title: Kali Recon of the Linux Web Victim
summary: A single-terminal reconnaissance run: the Kali attacker fingerprints the persistent Linux web victim at 10.93.0.10 — the loud nmap sweep and HTTP probes the SOC is meant to catch.
purpose: The baseline attacker workflow — establish position, confirm the target is up, enumerate services, fingerprint the web stack. It's the 'loud' activity a monitoring stack should surface, and the starting point most engagements build from.

steps.say: Confirm who and where we are on the attacker network (10.91.0.0/24).

steps.run: hostname && ip -4 addr show scope global | awk '/inet/{print $2}'

steps.say: Target: the persistent Linux web victim at 10.93.0.10. Is it up?

out: 64 bytes from 10.93.0.10: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.43 ms
64 bytes from 10.93.0.10: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.39 ms

steps.say: Service + version scan of the top ports (the loud step the SOC should catch).

out: Nmap done: 1 host up scanned in 7.41s

steps.say: Pull the landing page to confirm reachability end to end.
Recon complete. In Wazuh this run surfaces the nmap sweep and the HTTP probes.
